title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Modern libraries have (0) a remarkable transformation in recent decades. While they still (1) as places to borrow books, they now offer much more to their communities. Many libraries have (2) out into digital services, providing access to e-books, online databases, and streaming platforms. Computer facilities and free internet access help (3) the digital divide for those who cannot afford technology at home. Libraries have also (4) on the role of community centres, hosting events, workshops, and exhibitions. Some have even (5) aside spaces for co-working and small business development. Librarians are no longer just keepers of books; they now (6) as guides helping people navigate the vast world of information. Despite predictions that the internet would make libraries obsolete, they have (7) to be more resilient than expected. By constantly adapting to changing needs, libraries continue to (8) a vital role in education and community life around the world.

example.explanation: 'Undergone' is the correct verb to indicate that something has undergone a change.

questions.explanation: 'Serve as' is the correct expression to indicate the function of something.
'Branched out into' (with 'out into' already in the text) means to expand into new areas or activities.
'Bridge the divide' is the fixed expression for bridging differences.
'Taken on' (with 'on' already in the text) means taking on a new role or responsibility.
'Set aside' (with 'aside' already in the text) means to reserve something for a specific purpose.

'Act as' (with 'as' already in the text) is the correct expression to indicate that someone performs a role or function. 'Behave as' is less standard in professional/functional contexts, 'qualify as' implies meeting requirements to be so, and 'emerge as' adds a temporal nuance of becoming recognized as such.
'Proved to be' significa demostrar ser algo con el tiempo.
'Play a role' is the fixed expression for having importance in something.
